senses.definition: a physical or online location where people can place bets on various sporting events and other types of competitions, with odds determined by the bookmaker

examples.x: I stopped by the betting shop to place a bet on the horse race this afternoon.
He spends most of his weekends at the betting shop, hoping for a big win.
The betting shop was busy today, with people gathered around watching the game.
They opened a new betting shop in town, and it's already attracting a lot of customers.
I went to the betting shop with my friends to see if we could guess the winning team.

senses.definition: a place where people play and bet their money on gambling games

examples.x: The casino was filled with the sounds of slot machines and excited players.
They planned a weekend trip to the casino to celebrate their anniversary.
The luxurious casino featured high-stakes poker tables and gourmet restaurants.
Many people visit the casino hoping to win big jackpots.
The casino hosted a special event with live music and entertainment.

senses.definition: to finish a game without any winning sides

examples.x: Neither team could secure a victory, and the game was drawn at the end of regulation time.
The players chose to draw the round when no one could play a winning card.
The Monopoly game was drawn after several hours of intense trading and property management.
In the spirit of sportsmanship, players agreed to draw the round rather than prolonging the virtual battle.

senses.definition: the action or activity of betting on uncertain outcomes or playing games of chance, hoping to win money or prizes

examples.x: Gambling can be addictive, leading to financial troubles and emotional distress for many individuals.
Many countries have regulations in place to control and monitor gambling activities to protect consumers and prevent problem gambling.
Understanding the odds and knowing when to stop are essential aspects of responsible gambling.

senses.definition: a place where gambling activities are conducted illegally or secretly

examples.x: The police raided a gambling den hidden behind a local bar.
He lost all his money at a notorious gambling den in the city.
The underground gambling den was filled with high-stakes poker players.
They discovered a gambling den operating in the backroom of a restaurant.
The old building had been turned into a gambling den, attracting people from all over town.

senses.definition: the act of playing for stakes in the hope of winning, involves the payment of a price or consideration for the opportunity to win a prize or reward, which may include money, goods, or other valuable items
a form of illegal gambling that involves the selection of a series of numbers, often based on a publicly available source such as a lottery or race

examples.x: The police busted the operation that was running an illegal numbers game in the neighborhood.
He lost a lot of money trying his luck with the numbers game last week.
She was warned by her friend to stay away from the numbers game, as it could get dangerous.
He was caught up in a numbers game that promised big winnings but ended up with nothing.
The authorities are cracking down on the numbers game, hoping to put an end to underground gambling.

senses.definition: a gambling machine with a lever on the side that players pull after inserting coins in order to spin the reels

examples.x: The casino was filled with flashing lights and the sound of one-armed bandits.
He spent hours in front of the one-armed bandit, hoping for a big win.
Many people enjoy the thrill of playing the one-armed bandit in hopes of hitting the jackpot.
Online casinos offer a variety of one-armed bandit games for players to enjoy from the comfort of their homes.

senses.definition: a designated area within a casino or other gambling establishment where various table games, such as blackjack, craps, and roulette, are located

a type of savings bond issued by the government, in which the bondholder is entered into a monthly lottery drawing for the chance to win a tax-free cash prize, with the prize amounts ranging from small sums to much larger jackpots

examples.x: I bought some premium bonds last month, hoping to win a big prize.
The only way to win with premium bonds is through the monthly prize draw.
If you’re looking for an investment with no risk, premium bonds might be a good option.
I’ve been putting some of my savings into premium bonds instead of a regular savings account.
He was disappointed when his premium bonds didn’t win anything this month.

senses.definition: a single instance of betting, such as a roll of the dice in craps or a spin of the roulette wheel
a small card made of paper or cardstock that features concealed areas or panels that can be scratched off to reveal a prize or other information

examples.x: I bought a scratchcard at the store and won a small prize.
She likes to pick up a scratchcard whenever she goes to the convenience store.
We were excited when the scratchcard showed that we had won $50.
I’ve never won anything big on a scratchcard, just a few dollars here and there.

senses.definition: a type of contest or promotion in which entrants are selected at random to win a prize or prizes, often without any purchase or payment required to enter
a physical or electronic document that represents the purchase of a chance to win a prize or participate in a game
an unclear situation that either of two possibilities have an equal chance of happening

examples.x: It’s a toss-up whether we should go to the beach or stay home today.
With both candidates so evenly matched, it’s really a toss-up who will get the job.
It’s a toss-up whether I’ll make it to the meeting on time with this traffic.
The game was so close, it was a toss-up who would win.

senses.definition: to predict which side of a coin will face upwards after it is tossed

examples.x: He called heads and was thrilled when the coin landed on that side.
I called heads and won the coin toss.
She called tails but the coin landed on heads.
The captain called tails and won the coin toss.
We called tails and got the first possession.

senses.definition: a phrase that is often used in gambling to describe a bet in which the player has the option to double their winnings or lose everything, depending on the outcome of a subsequent event
a type of wager where two equal bets are placed on the same selection, one for the selection to win and the other for the selection to place
(gambling) a bet or wager that pays out at a 1:1 ratio, or in other words, a bet in which the potential payout is equal to the amount of the original wager

examples.x: The casino offered me even money on a blackjack hand, so I decided to take the risk.
If you bet $20 on this game, you’ll get $20 back if you win, since it’s even money.
He bet even money on the first round of the poker game and ended up doubling his chips.

senses.definition: bets or wagers with a relatively low chance of winning, but with the potential for a large payout if successful

examples.x: With long odds, no one expected the underdog to win the championship.
He bet on the long odds of the team making a comeback, and it turned out to be a winning bet.
The horse had long odds of winning the race, but it still managed to cross the finish line first.

senses.definition: referring to the likelihood of a particular event occurring, with a high probability of success or a low degree of uncertainty

examples.x: The favorite team has short odds to win the championship this year, so the payout for betting on them is not very high.
I decided to bet on the horse with short odds because I was pretty sure it would win.
Everyone is betting on the team with short odds, so I might try my luck with a long shot instead.

senses.definition: the likelihood of a particular event occurring, typically expressed as the ratio of the potential payout to the amount of the original wager

a type of gambling in which bettors place wagers on the outcome of an event based on a range of possible outcomes, with payouts determined by the accuracy of the bettor's prediction relative to the spread or range of outcomes set by the bookmaker

examples.x: He made a spread betting wager on the football match, guessing the team would win by more than 10 points.
She enjoys spread betting on horse races, betting on whether the winner will finish ahead by a certain number of lengths.
They discussed spread betting strategies, focusing on how to predict outcomes more accurately.
Spread betting is popular among people who like to take risks, as it offers the chance to win or lose big based on the result.

senses.definition: a pool betting system, in which all bets are pooled together, and the total amount is then divided among the winners, minus a commission taken by the bookmaker
a gambling term used to describe a situation where a player has lost all of their money that they had intended to gamble with, and is now in a negative financial position as a result
a casual or impulsive bet, often made on a whim or without much forethought
describing a situation where a large amount of money or other valuable assets are at risk, often in the context of gambling or other forms of high-risk activity
a bet or a monetary stake placed on the outcome of an event

examples.x: He placed a wager on his favorite team to win the match.
They made a friendly wager on who could finish the race first.
She lost the wager when her horse came in second place.
The casino offers various games where you can place a wager.
He made a wager with his friend about the outcome of the election.

senses.definition: to risk money on the result of a coming event by trying to predict it

examples.x: Friends often bet on sports events to make watching more exciting.
Some people enjoy going to the casino to bet on games of chance.
The group is currently betting on the outcome of the lottery.
Many enthusiasts are betting on horse races.
Last week, the group bet on the roulette wheel at the casino.

senses.definition: the amount of money or other valuable asset that a player risks or wagers on a particular bet or game
to place a bet on a particular outcome or event to occur

examples.x: He decided to back the underdog in the race, hoping for a big payout.
She backed the home team to win the championship.
They backed the favorite horse in the derby.
He backed the player to score the first goal in the match.
She backed the outcome of the election, confident in her prediction.

senses.definition: to place a bet on a particular outcome or event
a bet on a particular horse or team to finish in either first or second place, depending on the terms of the bet

examples.x: If you’re unsure about who will win, a place bet is a safer option.
The place bet paid out well because the horse came in second place, even though it didn’t win.
Instead of betting on the winner, I went for a place bet to cover the possibility of a close finish.
I had a place bet on the team to finish in the top three, so I was relieved when they came in second.

senses.definition: the act of flipping a coin to determine the outcome of a bet or wager
to combine multiple individual bets into one larger bet, with the potential for a higher payout if all of the individual bets are successful

examples.x: I wanted to parlay my $20 win into an even bigger payout with another bet.
Poker players will sometimes parlay a table stack into buying into a higher stakes game.

senses.definition: a person or organization that accepts bets on the outcome of sporting events, horse races, or other types of gambling activities

examples.x: I went to the bookie to place a bet on the big football game.
He lost a lot of money at the bookie's last weekend.
The bookie told me that I could win big if my team won by more than 10 points.
I used to go to a local bookie, but now I prefer to bet online.

senses.definition: a person who is highly skilled at manipulating playing cards for their own financial gain, often through cheating or deception

examples.x: The casino caught a card sharp using sleight of hand to cheat at poker.
He was known as a card sharp in the local poker scene, always winning with suspiciously perfect hands.
The dealer warned the players about the card sharp who had been banned from other casinos.
At the high-stakes game, a card sharp was caught trying to mark the cards to improve his chances.
He had the reputation of being a card sharp, and no one wanted to play against him because they knew it wasn’t fair.

senses.definition: a person who works in a casino or other gambling establishment, responsible for dealing cards, managing bets, and facilitating the game for players

examples.x: The croupier dealt the cards with precision, making sure everyone had a fair chance.
The croupier smiled and asked if I wanted to place another bet.
After a few rounds, the croupier began to count the chips on the table.
The croupier explained the rules of blackjack to the new players at the table.

senses.definition: a person who participates in games of chance or bets on uncertain outcomes, often with the aim of winning money or other prizes

examples.x: The gambler spent hours at the poker table hoping to win big.
He is a professional gambler who specializes in blackjack.
The casino was filled with eager gamblers trying their luck.
Some gamblers see betting as a fun hobby, while others take it very seriously.
The gambler studied the odds carefully before placing his next bet.

senses.definition: a person who bets on very large sums of money in casinos

examples.x: The renowned high roller placed a million-dollar bet on the roulette table, drawing a crowd of onlookers.
Known as a high roller in the poker world, he frequently wagered tens of thousands of dollars in high-stakes games.
The casino offers exclusive privileges to its high rollers, including private gaming rooms and luxury accommodations.
As a high roller, she spent a weekend in Las Vegas gambling and living the high life.
While most visitors played conservatively, the high roller at the blackjack table bet $50,000 on a single hand.

senses.definition: bets or wagers made by experienced or professional bettors who have a reputation for making successful bets due to their access to insider information, advanced statistical models, or other tools and resources
the odds or price at which a horse or other competitor is valued at the start of a race or event, as determined by the bookmaker or betting exchange

examples.x: The horse was a favorite at the starting price, but it finished in fourth place.
I decided to bet on the long shot, even though the starting price was quite high.
The starting price for the race was set just before the horses lined up at the starting gate.
They announced the starting price of each horse after all the bets were in.
Even though the starting price was low, the underdog shocked everyone by winning.

senses.definition: the amount of money or other valuable asset that a player or bettor receives as a result of a successful bet or wager
a person who deals with the funds or money in a game, typically in gambling or banking games, and may also refer to the dealer or player responsible for controlling the bank or funds
a blackjack rule where if a player is dealt five cards without busting, they automatically win the hand, regardless of the dealer's hand
a hand in blackjack that does not contain an Ace or contains an Ace that can only be counted as 1, making it inflexible in its value

examples.x: I got a hard hand of 12, so I wasn't sure if I should hit or stay.
He had a hard hand in blackjack, with a total of 16, so he decided to stand.
The dealer showed a 6, and I had a hard hand of 17, so I chose to stand.
With a hard hand of 19, I felt confident enough to stick with my cards.
He made a risky move with a hard hand of 13, hoping to get a lucky card.

senses.definition: a type of instant win game or gambling ticket, typically made of paper, with concealed windows that can be opened to reveal if the player has won a prize

examples.x: I bought a pull tab at the gas station, hoping for a quick win.
She scratched off the cover of the pull tab and realized she had won the jackpot.
Pull tabs are a fun and easy way to try your luck without spending much money.
The charity fundraiser sold pull tabs as part of their event to raise funds.
I was excited to see if my pull tab would match the winning symbols.

senses.definition: a type of mechanical arcade game and gambling device that originated in Japan, in which players shoot small metal balls into a vertical playing field filled with pins, bumpers, and other obstacles

examples.x: I spent the afternoon playing pachinko at the arcade with my friends.
She won a lot of balls in the pachinko game, and it was so exciting to watch.
After work, he likes to relax by playing pachinko at the local parlor.
The noise from all the pachinko machines was overwhelming as soon as I entered the arcade.
He exchanged his winnings from the pachinko game for a few cool prizes.

senses.definition: an arcade machine that has a claw-like device used to grab prizes inside the machine and drop them into a chute for players to win

examples.x: I spent most of my allowance at the arcade trying to win a stuffed bear from the claw machine.
After several failed attempts, I finally grabbed a toy car from the claw machine.
The claw machine in the mall is always so tempting; I can never walk by without trying it.
My younger brother won a cute plushie from the claw machine on his first try.
I tried to get a stuffed dinosaur from the claw machine, but it kept slipping out of the claw's grip.
